name: accessibility-check
description: Evaluate accessibility and WCAG compliance of UI components and pages. Use when the user asks to "check accessibility", "audit a11y", "WCAG review", "screen reader test", or needs to verify inclusive design practices.
capabilities: Evaluate UI components against WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA criteria
Identify missing alt text, labels, and semantic HTML issues
Check color contrast ratios for readability
Review keyboard navigation and focus indicators
Provide code examples for common accessibility fixes
author: Dami-Randle
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing WCAG 2.1 guidelines and accessibility audit templates. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, or external commands are present. The skill operates solely through prompt-based content delivery. All 42 static findings are false positives from the scanner misinterpreting markdown formatting and documentation content as security threats.
valueStatement: This skill evaluates UI components and pages against WCAG 2.1 guidelines. It identifies accessibility issues and provides specific fixes. Use it to ensure your interfaces are usable by people with disabilities.
limitations: Cannot run automated testing tools like axe-core or pa11y
Cannot test actual screen reader output or keyboard navigation
Requires manual verification of automated audit results
Does not perform visual testing across zoom levels

useCases.title: Code review accessibility
useCases.description: Review React components for WCAG compliance before merging pull requests

useCases.title: Design system audit
useCases.description: Audit component libraries for accessibility gaps and inclusive design issues

useCases.title: Compliance verification
useCases.description: Verify products meet accessibility requirements for legal compliance

promptTemplates.title: Quick component check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check accessibility of this button component. Is it keyboard accessible? Does it have proper focus states?

promptTemplates.title: Form validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit this form for accessibility issues. Check labels, error messages, and keyboard navigation.

promptTemplates.title: Full page review
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit of this page. Check perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Integration check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run both the accessibility check and UX audit on this dashboard. Focus on contrast, navigation, and ARIA usage.

bestPractices: Run accessibility checks before every feature release
Combine automated testing tools with manual keyboard testing
Include people with disabilities in user testing when possible
antiPatterns: Using placeholder text instead of proper form labels
Relying solely on color to convey information
Skipping accessibility testing until the end of development

faq.question: What WCAG levels does this skill check?
faq.answer: This skill covers WCAG 2.1 Level A and Level AA criteria, the standard targets for most compliance requirements.

faq.question: Does this replace automated testing tools?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidelines and code reviews. Use axe-core or pa11y for automated testing in your CI pipeline.

faq.question: Can I use this with Playwright tests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill references Playwright integration for automated accessibility testing alongside manual review.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reviews code you provide. No data is stored or transmitted to external services.

faq.question: Why did my accessibility audit fail?
faq.answer: Common causes include missing alt text, low color contrast, missing form labels, and missing focus indicators. Review the specific issues listed.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual testing?
faq.answer: This skill identifies code-level issues. Manual keyboard and screen reader testing catches runtime behavior that code review cannot.
